question: Can a small predeclared entry-selectivity family for the current one-shot BTCUSDT Donchian/ATR strategy remain positive after market-order costs and actual funding in sequential development, evaluation, and confirmation periods while holding all exit parameters fixed?
development_gate: trades >= 100; base and stress mean net return per trade > 0; at least two of 2021/2022/2023 base means > 0; worst annual base mean >= -0.001; rank by worst annual base mean, stress mean, base mean, then deterministic selectivity tie-break; keep at most 3
evaluation_gate: trades >= 60; base and stress means > 0; 2024 and 2025 base means > 0; same-direction base mean exceeds 20/2/0.5 by at least 0.0005; first development-ranked passer only
confirmation_gate: trades >= 15; base and stress means > 0; combined evaluation plus confirmation base mean > 0
known_exposure: The underlying 2021-2026H1 public BTCUSDT data and an older 20/2/0.5, 96x15m proxy have been exposed. The locked current 4x15m exit and full 72-configuration entry matrix have not previously been executed in the repository.
allowed_fixes_after_checkpoint: parsing, timestamp, checksum and data-quality defects
implementation defects that do not alter search space, periods, costs, execution order, exit rules, ranks or gates
forbidden_after_checkpoint: adding or removing parameter values after viewing results
changing costs, periods, gates or tie-breaks
optimizing stop, take-profit, fraction or hold duration
reading evaluation before development candidates are fixed
reading confirmation before one evaluation candidate is fixed
study_code_revision_reason: Before any configuration result existed, detect the open_time header present in newer Binance monthly CSV archives while retaining headerless parsing for older archives. No research rule, parameter, period, cost, ordering, rank or gate changed.
